message:
feat(server): add async runtimes to background workers, optimize cleanup and TUI rendering

Background worker threads (ack_ttl, cleanup, retention, tool_metrics, tui_poller,
HTTP supervisor) now spawn dedicated single-threaded async runtimes via
asupersync::RuntimeBuilder. This ensures each worker has a proper async context
available, preventing panics when async primitives are used inside these threads.

The file reservation cleanup module now uses git's native pathspec matching
instead of spawning a git process per matched file, significantly reducing
process overhead during stale reservation detection.

The disk monitor loop now checks for shutdown and sleeps BEFORE doing work on
each iteration, preventing a wasted monitoring cycle on shutdown and ensuring
the first sleep happens before the first check (matching the intended design).

TUI rendering gains a cached composite path (render_cached) that reuses the
previously computed ambient effect buffer when the simulation tick has not
advanced, avoiding redundant plasma/particle computation on intermediate frames.

Additional fixes:
- /mail/api/locks now accepts trailing slash (route parity)
- reply_message arguments are now normalized alongside send_message
- "resource" added to allowed URL schemes in HTML sanitizer
- saturating_mul used for TTL microsecond conversions to prevent overflow
- Removed unused sqlmodel_core::Value import from tui_app
- .clone() used instead of .to_string() for already-owned recipient strings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
